meta_title: For Freelancers: email automation to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Email Automation to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage email automation to overcome a lack of design skills. A high-impact, intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop struggling with pixel-perfect layouts. By integrating Antigravity for structured data handling and Claude Code for rapid infrastructure deployment, you can automate professional, high-converting email sequences. This workflow replaces manual design with programmatic, brand-consistent templates, allowing you to ship client projects in hours rather than weeks.
content.intro:
The client meeting went well, but the 'design' requirement looms like a storm cloud. You’re a developer, not a graphic designer, and your CSS grid skills aren't going to save you from a three-week slog of tweaking email margins. The pressure to ship high-end, automated marketing funnels without a creative agency budget is the silent killer of freelance profitability.

But here is the secret: nobody cares about your design skills; they care about their conversion rate. By treating email templates as code rather than art, you can build modular, automated systems that outperform hand-crafted designs every time. We are going to bypass the design phase entirely and build a robust, AI-driven automation engine that scales.

steps.title: Define the Schema
steps.description: Establish a rigid data structure for your emails to ensure consistency. This prevents design drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a transactional email template. Include fields for headline, body_text, cta_url, and brand_color_hex.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file schema.json --task 'generate validation logic for this email schema'

steps.title: Build the Logic Engine
steps.description: Construct the backend logic that injects dynamic data into your templates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a Node.js function that maps the email schema into an MJML template structure. Ensure responsive design defaults.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'npm install mjml' --task 'implement injection script'

steps.title: Automate the Delivery Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your logic to an ESP using a secure API integration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust integration module for Resend API to handle batch sending of the generated MJML templates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'connect logic engine to Resend API with error handling'

steps.title: Implement AI-Powered Copywriting
steps.description: Use Claude to refine the email copy based on the target audience persona.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a prompt template for Claude to rewrite email body_text for higher click-through rates based on AIDA framework.
steps.claude_code_command: claude chat --file body_text.txt --instruction 'rewrite this for high CTR using AIDA'

steps.title: Testing and Deployment
steps.description: Automate the testing of templates across multiple devices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite that sends preview emails to a list of testing addresses and validates responsive breakpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'execute email test suite and log results'

pro_tips: Always use MJML for email; never write raw HTML to avoid the 'Outlook rendering nightmare'.
Use CSS variables in your templates so clients can change brand colors in one line of config.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode that logs emails to a local JSON file instead of sending them to avoid API costs during development.
Tag your emails with metadata to track which specific template version converted better.
Never hardcode copy; always pull strings from a translation or content file to allow non-technical clients to edit their own text.
content.next_steps: Deploy your automation to a serverless function (like AWS Lambda or Vercel) to trigger emails based on database webhooks.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop trading time for manual design tasks and start selling high-value automated systems. Stop building from scratch and start building for scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
